title: Add financial transaction amount comparison
description: Implements amount comparison logic for detecting duplicate transactions using floating point comparison.
diff:
diff --git a/finance/transaction_matcher.py b/finance/transaction_matcher.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c5d6e7f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/finance/transaction_matcher.py
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+def is_duplicate_transaction(amount1: float, amount2: float, fee1: float, fee2: float) -> bool:
+    '''Check if two transactions are duplicates by comparing amounts.'''
+    total1 = amount1 + fee1
+    total2 = amount2 + fee2
+    return total1 == total2  # Floating point comparison!
+
+def calculate_balance(transactions: list[float]) -> float:
+    '''Calculate running balance from transaction amounts.'''
+    balance = 0.0
+    for amount in transactions:
+        balance += amount  # Accumulates floating point errors
+    return balance
comments: vera: CRITICAL: Direct floating point equality comparison for financial amounts! 0.1 + 0.2 != 0.3 in IEEE 754. This will produce false negatives, missing actual duplicate transactions. Use Decimal for all financial calculations or compare integer cents.
walt: The cumulative balance calculation also accumulates floating point errors over thousands of transactions. After 10K transactions, the balance could be off by several cents.
author-xena: I'll switch to Decimal with proper quantization.
author: xena
